political.s: Via parent company General Motors: FEC: $78K PAC donations; 100% to Republican, 0% to Democratic committees.  [CA CalAccess + NY COELIG + TX Ethics adds state-level totals.]

labor.s: Via parent company General Motors: 133 federal penalties totaling $2.2B since 2000 (Violation Tracker).

charity.s: $50M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.03% of revenue) via General Motors Foundation.

environment.s: Via parent company General Motors: InfluenceMap LobbyMap grade C: mixed climate-policy engagement (Strategic engagement, Mixed topline).

dei.s: Via parent company General Motors: HRC Corporate Equality Index 100/100 (2025) — top-tier LGBTQ+ workplace equality rating. Bloomberg Gender-Equality Index member (2024) — transparent on female leadership, equal pay, inclusive culture.

privacy.s: Via parent company General Motors: CFPB consumer complaints: 9221 on record (100 in past 12mo); 100% timely response rate.

execPay.s: Via parent company General Motors: CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio 333:1 (median employee pay $89,785) — SEC proxy statement (DEF 14A, 2026).

wiki.extract: DEXRON is the trade name for a group of technical specifications for automatic transmission fluid (ATF) created by General Motors (GM). The name was first registered as a trademark and later evolved into a brand of GM. GM licenses the name and specifications to companies that manufacture the fluid and sell it under their own brand names. Not all DEXRON fluids are licensed by GM for reselling under another brand name. To be licensed, the product must have a license number that begins with the letters B through J and include a "DEXRON Approved" sticker on its container. Like many automobile manu
